description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a weathered, natural appearance and a pale, off-white coloration with subtle tan and grayish patches. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring a prominent cavity or depression on one side, likely formed by natural erosion or breakage. The stone's form is asymmetrical, with one side appearing more angular and the other more rounded, and it has several small crevices and pitted areas. The texture is coarse, with visible grain and some areas showing signs of abrasion or wear. The stone is solid and dense, with no visible artificial modifications or carvings. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a substantial obstacle or barrier in an urban or park setting.
description: A large, irregular, pale stone with a rough, weathered surface and a prominent cavity on one side. Its coarse texture and asymmetrical shape make it a natural-looking urban obstacle.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's prominent cavity and rough, pitted surface, with tan and grayish hues visible.
description_view_1: The stone appears more angular from this angle, with the cavity less visible and the surface showing more pronounced textural variation.
description_view_2: This side displays the broadest, flattest surface of the stone, with subtle color mottling and minimal visible cavities.
description_view_3: The stone's rounded edge and smoother, lighter-toned surface are most apparent here, with the cavity partially obscured.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and subtle color variations, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible signs of natural erosion and surface abrasion, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping elements such as grass, soil, or other stones.
